Article Title: Senior BJP leader, former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passes away at 88
Article Text: BENGALURU: Senior BJP leader and former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passed away at the age of 88 at a private hospital in Bengaluru on Tuesday.A veteran leader, Gowda was associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for several decades.Former Karnataka chief minister and BJP leader BS Yediyurappa expressed grief over the death of senior BJP leader and former minister Ramachandra Gowda, describing him as a "brother" and a longtime companion.In a post on X, Yediyurappa recalled their journey together since the Jana Sangh era, their efforts to build the party in Karnataka, and praised Gowda's administrative acumen and dedication as a minister.BS Yediyurappa tweets, "I have lost yet another brother. The news of the passing of Shri Ramachandra Gowda, my soulmate of decades, senior leader, and former minister, has brought me immense sorrow. The days of struggle—standing shoulder to shoulder since the Jana Sangh era, toiling day and night, building the party from the grassroots level together in the state—swirl before my eyes. As a colleague in my cabinet, I have witnessed his administrative acumen and dedication up close. "
Article Summary: Senior BJP leader and former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passed away at the age of 88. Gowda was associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for several decades. Former Karnataka chief minister and BJP leader BS Yediyurappa expressed grief over the death.

Article Title: BLO abused, obstructed during SIR survey in Delhi’s Chandni Mahal; 4 arrested
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Delhi Police has arrested four men for allegedly obstructing a booth-level officer (BLO) and misbehaving with her while she was carrying out duties related to the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in the Chandni Mahal area earlier this month.According to police, a case was registered at Chandni Mahal police station on the complaint of a postgraduate teacher (special education), who was deployed as a BLO during the SIR exercise at Fatak Telian on July 5.The complainant alleged that while she was performing her official duties, one of the accused placed the BLO register on her lap and signed it despite her objection. She further alleged that several other persons present at the spot abused her, refused to accept SIR forms, and behaved in a manner that humiliated her and prevented her from discharging her official responsibilities.Police said the incident was reported on July 15 after the complainant approached the additional district magistrate (ADM), who informed the police authorities. The complainant told investigators that the delay in reporting the matter was due to consultations with her senior officers regarding the appropriate course of action.Following verification of the complaint and examination of the available material, police said a prima facie case was established.An FIR was subsequently registered under Sections 221 (voluntarily obstructing any public servant), 79 (word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman), 132 (assault or criminal force to deter a public servant from discharging his duty), 74 (assault or criminal force to a woman with intent to outrage her modesty), and 3(5) (common intention) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS).The four accused — Mohammad Sabreen (44), Ateeq ur Rehman (58), Mohammad Asif (50), and Mohammad Aftab (44) — all residents of the Turkman Gate area, have been arrested, police said.
Article Summary: Delhi Police has arrested four men for allegedly obstructing a booth-level officer. The officer was carrying out duties related to the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. The complainant alleged that while she was performing her official duties, one of the accused placed the BLO register on her lap and signed it despite her objection.

Article Title: Girl’s rape: HC cancels teacher’s bail
Article Text:
Photo for representation





New Delhi: Days after it cancelled the bail plea of a school staffer accused of raping a minor, Delhi High Court Wednesday cancelled the bail granted to a woman teacher in the same case.Noting that Pocso cases warrant “utmost caution”, the court asked her to surrender in three days, in connection with the alleged rape of a three-year-old girl by a staff member inside a private school in west Delhi’s Janakpuri. Last month, HC had set aside the bail granted to the main accused, the school caretaker.Justice Saurabh Banerjee Wednesday observed that while considering bail in such cases, “essential surrounding factors” like physical, emotional, mental and psychological harm to the survivor had to be given due weightage.It was dealing with a plea by Delhi Police challenging the trial court order that granted bail to the teacher on May 20. The teacher was arrested for allegedly concealing the incident.The trial court wrongly granted relief to the teacher merely because the survivor did not disclose her name in the complaint, HC said. Emphasising that any three-year-old cannot be expected to reveal every detail while making the initial complaint, Justice Banerjee said the trial court erred in ignoring the fact that the survivor had not only identified the teacher in the presence of her mother but also the incident spot.
Article Summary: Delhi High Court cancels bail granted to a woman teacher in the same case. Court asked her to surrender in three days, in connection with the alleged rape of a three-year-old girl by a staff member inside a private school in west Delhi's Janakpuri.

Article Title: Woman alleges abuse, asault by bike taxi rider
Article Text:
New Delhi:





A 27-year-old woman from Manipur has alleged that a bike taxi rider associated with a cab aggregator assaulted and abused her during a ride in May. Police registered a case this month and have bound down the rider, Sushil Kumar (24), claiming the dispute stemmed from an argument over speeding and the fare. The rider allegedly made unnecessary stops to buy tobacco, repeatedly spat, some of which landed on her feet, and drove slowly. When she objected, he allegedly dropped her before her destination, abused her, slapped her, and left without collecting the fare. She also alleged that the rider repeatedly called her and sent abusive messages after the incident.
Article Summary: A 27-year-old woman from Manipur has alleged that a bike taxi rider assaulted and abused her during a ride in May. Police registered a case this month and have bound down the rider, Sushil Kumar (24) The rider allegedly made unnecessary stops to buy tobacco, repeatedly spat, some of which landed on her feet, and drove slowly.

Article Title: 8-year-old schoolgirl injured in hit-and-run by motorcyclist riding in the wrong direction in Mumbai
Article Text:
Eight-year-old girl suffered serious injuries after a motorcyclist hit her.





Mumbai: An eight-year-old schoolgirl suffered serious injuries after being hit by a motorcyclist riding in the wrong direction at Jogeshwari West on Wednesday. Amboli police has registered a case against an unknown person for rash driving.Around 9.40 am on July 15, Reva Pawar, 8, was walking down to her tuitions with her mother, Nidhi. While they were crossing the road near the divider, opposite Cafe Arfa, a motorcycle riding in the wrong direction hit Reva. As a result, she left her mother's hand and got dragged onto the street. She hurt her head, hands, legs and waist and lost consciousness.Meanwhile the motorcyclist that had hit her also skidded and fell. But he and his pillion got back on the two-wheeler and fled without taking Reva to the hospital. Nidhi told the police that the motorcyclist was between 25 and 30 years of age and dressed in a red shirt. Police have found CCTV grabs of the motorcyclist and are looking for him.
Article Summary: Reva Pawar, 8, was walking down to her tuitions with her mother, Nidhi. While they were crossing the road near the divider, opposite Cafe Arfa, a motorcycle riding in the wrong direction hit Reva. Police have found CCTV grabs of the motorcyclist and are looking for him.

Article Title: 3,132 birth certificate corrections made in Mumbai’s M-East ward without supporting documents, applications: BMC in RTI reply
Article Text:
Based on the RTI findings, Kirit Somaiya has alleged that the corrections in the birth certificates were carried out illegally and has demanded that the BMC register an police complaint to investigate the matter and identify those responsible

Mumbai:





The M-East ward office in Govandi accounted for 3,132 birth certificate corrections that were made without supporting documents or applications, according to an RTI reply from the BMC received by former BJP MP Kirit Somaiya.The RTI response has also revealed that across the city, 19,753 birth certificates were issued without any supporting documents, while a total 86,020 birth record corrections were carried out in BMC’s SAP system between 2024 and 2026.In its RTI reply, BMC’s public health department stated that based on a revised list received from the information technology department, 86,020 birth record corrections were made in the SAP system. After verification, officials found that 6,109 correction records were duplicate entries, indicating the same birth records had been corrected more than once.The RTI response further stated that 19,753 birth certificates were issued without any supporting documents or applications being available with the civic administration. Of these, 14,194 were issued from the M-East ward office, while the remaining certificates were issued from L, E, and M-West wards.Based on the RTI findings, Somaiya has alleged that the corrections were carried out illegally and has demanded that the BMC retrieve all 86,020 corrected birth certificates and register an police complaint to investigate the matter and identify those responsible.The BMC’s RTI reply, however, does not describe the corrections as illegal. It only states the number of corrected records, notes the duplicate entries, and mentions that supporting documents were unavailable in thousands of cases.
Article Summary: The M-East ward office in Govandi accounted for 3,132 birth certificate corrections that were made without supporting documents or applications. The RTI response has also revealed that across the city, 19,753 birth certificates were issued without any supporting documents. A total 86,020 birth record corrections were carried out in BMC’s SAP system.

Article Title: Unrest within Sena & BJP along with NCP, says MPCC chief Sakpal
Article Text:
Maharashtra Pradesh Congress Committee (MPCC) chief Harshwardhan Sapkal

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





Maharashtra Pradesh Congress Committee (MPCC) chief Harshwardhan Sapkal on Tuesday said that unrest was brewing within Shiv Sena and BJP along with the NCP.Referring to NCP leader challenging deputy CM Sunetra Pawar’s election as party’s national president, Sapkal said, “The balance between Mahayuti is disturbed and the unrest within the three alliance partners will soon come to the fore. ”Sapkal, while addressing media persons at Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, alleged that the state govt failed to prepare for poor monsoon and its impact on parts of Maharashtra.“The CM and other ruling party leaders only made speeches about the mitigation measures for El Niño impact. However, there is no preparedness at the ground-level yet. No policies have been made for reserving water for drinking needs. The farmers are supplied with bogus seeds in many parts,” he said.Referring to the loan waiver scheme announced for the farmers by the state govt, Sapkal said the roll out of the scheme is deliberately delayed. “After providing multiple reasons for implementing the scheme, the government is now seeking income tax details of the farmers. Some ruling party leaders are trying hard so that the desired details become unavailable,” he said.Sapkal also said that the RSS should show courage and expel its “members” involved in the Ram temple donation theft at Ayodhya. “The RSS in the past was feeling proud that its members were at the helm of affairs at the Ram temple trust. Now, when robbery takes place on the donation, RSS cannot only express grief,” he said.Sapkal said the party would intensify its agitation on different public issues and strengthen the network in days to come.
Article Summary: MPCC chief Harshwardhan Sapkal said unrest was brewing within Shiv Sena and BJP along with the NCP. He alleged that the state govt failed to prepare for poor monsoon and its impact on parts of Maharashtra. He said the party would intensify its agitation on different public issues and strengthen the network in days to come.

Article Title: Kabaddi coach held for sexual assault of 17-yr-old girl
Article Text:
In Custody

Chennai





: A 29-year-old local Kabaddi coach, who had been absconding for nearly three weeks, was arrested by the Semmancheri police in Kodaikanal in connection with the sexual assault case of a 17-year-old girl.The accused, identified as Raji ,29, who runs a kabbadi club, had been evading police arrest since the Semmancheri All Women Police Station (AWPS) booked him under the POCSO Act In June, teammates of the trainee noticed a sudden change in her behaviour and visible discomfort, police said. The survivor, who had lost both her parents, was residing with her grandmother. Taking advantage of the grandmother’s brief absence from the house, the coach sexually assaulted her at her residence, police said. Based on the complaints, the Semmancheri AWPS registered an FIR against the coach on the charges of sexual assault and aggravated sexual assault.
Article Summary: The accused, identified as Raji,29, who runs a kabbadi club, had been evading police arrest since the Semmancheri All Women Police Station (AWPS) booked him under the POCSO Act. In June, teammates of the trainee noticed a sudden change in her behaviour and visible discomfort.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Kanyakumari custodial death case: 3 wardens arrested
Article Text:
TOI

TOI





MADURAI: The Kanyakumari district police on Tuesday arrested two jail wardens and the chief jail warden of Nagercoil sub-jail for attacking a 35-year-old remand prisoner, leading to his death on Monday.The arrested were jail wardens Sivakumar , 48, of Swamiyarmadam, and Jegan , 46, of Thengaipattinam, and chief warden Thirumalai Nambi Suresh, 50, of Kalakkad.A senior police officer toldthat the deceased, Sabari Varman, was an alcoholic who behaved ‘abnormally’. Though he wasn’t drunk that day, he reportedly provoked other inmates. In order to bring him under control, one of the arrested jail staff allegedly beat him with his hands. The others were arrested for concealing evidence.“We have also identified five inmates who assisted in the crime. Necessary action will be taken against them after further investigation,” the officer added. Though autopsy on the body was done on Tuesday, his family is yet to receive the body. The officer said preliminary observation during autopsy revealed that injuries were present in his body.Sabari Varman, a disabled person from Ethankadu, was lodged in the Nagercoil sub-jail on July 9 in a case connected with the possession of 200 grams of gutka.He died on Monday after developing health issues, according to prison authorities. The family alleged custodial torture and held a road roko in front of Kanyakumari medical college hospital. A case was registered by the Nesamani Nagar police and an investigation is underway.A senior police officer told, “When he was remanded, the medical fitness by doctors didn’t mention any external injuries; the same was observed in the judicial magistrate’s statement.” The officer said the victim’s sister had mentioned in her complaint that he was doing well when she had visited him in prison.Meanwhile, DMK accused the TVK govt of engaging in diversionary politics. Party legal wing joint secretary I Paranthamen said, “While this incident was unfolding in Kanyakumari, the chief minister was busy filming scenes — such as touring the streets of Perambur and distributing rice at a ration shop — to keep the cameras focused on himself and divert attention from the actual event.”
Article Summary: The arrested were jail wardens Sivakumar and Jegan and chief warden Thirumalai Nambi Suresh. The deceased, Sabari Varman, was an alcoholic who behaved ‘abnormally’ Though he wasn’t drunk that day, he reportedly provoked other inmates.

Article Title: Solar scam: Kerala HC quashes case against Tenny Joppan
Article Text:
Tenny Joppan

Kochi

:





High court has allowed a petition filed by Tenny Joppan, former personal assistant to late chief minister Oommen Chandy , and quashed the criminal proceedings against him in the Solar scam case registered on the complaint of entrepreneur Mallelil Sreedharan Nair.A bench of Justice C S Dias allowed the petition in view of Nair’s submission that the dispute which led to the case had been amicably settled.The case arose from a complaint lodged by Nair in 2013 alleging that Saritha S Nair and Biju Radhakrishnan had cheated him of Rs 40 lakh on the promise of setting up a solar power plant. The allegation against Joppan was that he had facilitated the fraud by persuading Nair to hand over money to the first and second accused. Joppan was arrested in June 2013 and spent 50 days in judicial custody before being released on bail. The case later snowballed into a major political controversy, with allegations being levelled against Chandy and several other politicians.The case is presently pending before the JFCM Court II, Pathanamthitta, at the stage of framing of charges.
Article Summary: The case arose from a complaint lodged by entrepreneur Mallelil Sreedharan Nair. He alleged that Saritha S Nair and Biju Radhakrishnan had cheated him of Rs 40 lakh. Joppan was arrested in June 2013 and spent 50 days in judicial custody.

Article Title: Pending PILs: Kerala HC restrains state waqf board from taking major decisions
Article Text:
Kochi:





High court on Wednesday, observing that the constitution of the Kerala State Waqf Board was prima facie not in conformity with the amended Waqf Act, 2025, ordered that the present board shall not take any major or policy decisions or incur any capital expenditure without its permission.A bench of Chief Justice Soumen Sen and Justice V M Syam Kumar passed the order while considering four PILs, including one filed by BJP leader Shone George, challenging the newly constituted Kerala State Waqf Board for the non-inclusion of two non-Muslim members as mandated under the amended Act. In the remaining three petitions, Stalin Devan challenged the inclusion of former MLA Kunhammed Kutty, a CPM functionary, as a board member, while Sajid Hussain Khatai of Fort Kochi challenged the non-inclusion of a Shia member. Meanwhile, a petition filed by the Assembly of Christian Trust Services (ACTS) challenged not only the absence of non-Muslim members but also the inclusion of the disputed 404-acre Munambam land in the UMEED portal, the central govt’s database of waqf properties.The petitioners also questioned the inclusion of the additional secretary of law department as an ex officio member of the board, contending that it violated the statutory requirement, which envisages the nomination of a joint secretary to the govt from a department dealing with waqf matters.Revenue department under secretary B Pushpavally filed a statement on behalf of the state govt stating that the govt was prepared to reconstitute the board strictly in compliance with the amended Section 14 of the Act.Counsel for the board contended that the two non-Muslim members could not be nominated in view of the pendency of matters relating to the amended Waqf Act before the Supreme Court. However, the additional solicitor general disputed the contention, submitting that no such issue was pending before the apex court. After considering the rival submissions, the court observed that the admitted non-inclusion of two non-Muslim members in the board, coupled with the non-inclusion of a Shia member, as alleged by one of the petitioners, prima facie indicated that the constitution of the present board was not in conformity with the provisions of the Act. HC posted the petitions to July 22.
Article Summary: Kerala State Waqf Board ordered not to take any major or policy decisions or incur any capital expenditure without its permission. A bench of Chief Justice Soumen Sen and Justice V M Syam Kumar passed the order while considering four PILs, including one filed by BJP leader Shone George.

Article Title: Police arrest man for abetment to suicide of woman
Article Text:
Kottayam

:





Police have booked a man on charges of abetment to suicide in connection with the death of a 29-year-old woman, whose body was found hanging inside her rented house near Adoor on Monday.The accused, Arun Kumar , 34, of Parakkode, Ezhamkulam, who was under observation at Adoor police station since the incident, was arrested, produced before a court and remanded.The woman was found hanging from the stair railing inside the house around 7pm. Neighbours who heard a commotion from the house rushed in and found her hanging; Kumar was present in the house at the time. On noticing bloodstains on her body and on Kumar’s clothes, neighbours alerted police, who took him to station and registered a case of unnatural death.The following day, a team including a scientific expert, a fingerprint expert and a police photographer from Pathanamthitta district police headquarters collected evidence from the scene. Victim’s body was shifted to Govt Medical College Hospital, Kottayam, where a forensic surgeon conducted postmortem.Investigators said Kumar had recorded her private videos and photographs on his phone. Police said he pressed her to marry him and to convert her religion, which she resisted. Following this, he allegedly harassed her and threatened to circulate the private recordings on the social media.Based on probe findings, police approached court to reclassify the case from unnatural death to abetment to suicide and named Kumar as the accused.
Article Summary: Police have booked a man on charges of abetment to suicide in connection with the death of a 29-year-old woman, whose body was found hanging inside her rented house near Adoor on Monday. Arun Kumar, 34, of Parakkode, Ezhamkulam, was arrested, produced before a court and remanded.
